API Keys & Configuration
Enter your keys once here — they auto-populate across all tabs. Keys are stored only in your browser (localStorage), never sent to any server.
Get started in 3 steps
1
Add an API Key
Get Claude free at console.anthropic.com — powers all AI features
2
Fill Business Profile
Business name, phone, address, city, website — every tab pulls from here
3
Come back here
Your SEO score, insights, and action plan will be waiting for you
Dashboard auto-updates ✓
Anthropic (Claude AI) AI emails · content · descriptions
Used for AI-generated outreach emails in the Backlinks tab and content generation in Web 2.0. Get a key at console.anthropic.com → API Keys. Pay-per-use, approximately $0.003 per request.
Not set
Yext Knowledge API 150+ directory sync · Bing Places
Syncs your business NAP to 150+ directories simultaneously including Bing Places and Apple Maps. Get your key at hitchhikers.yext.com → API Keys. Requires Yext subscription.
Not set
Not set
Google OAuth Google Business Profile API
Connects to Google Business Profile to read and update your verified listing. Create a project at console.cloud.google.com → enable "My Business Business Information API" → create OAuth 2.0 Web Client. Apply for API access →
Not set
Not connected
Open OAuth Playground — Gmail API v1 — tick gmail.send + gmail.readonly — Authorize — Exchange code — copy Access token — paste above. Tokens last ~1 hour.
IndexNow & Optional Keys
IndexNow is a free protocol that instantly notifies Bing, Yandex, and other engines when content changes. Generate a key at indexnow.org. Create a text file with your key at yourdomain.com/[key].txt
Not set
Optional
Citation Submission — BrightLocal API
Not set
Not set
BrightLocal's Citation Builder API submits to 60+ directories automatically. This replaces the manual simulation — real NAP data is submitted, listings go live, and you can track status from their dashboard. Paid plans from $29/month. Free trial available.
Backlink Checker APIs
Not set
Not set
Moz free tier: 10 requests/month. Provides real Domain Authority (DA) scores for any URL. Ahrefs and SEMrush can also be added if you have paid plans — their APIs work the same way.
Not set
Not set

Submit Your Website — Free Backlink Sources
Manually vetted, zero spam. Each platform is editorially reviewed and indexes submitted content. Click to open the submission page with your details pre-filled in the clipboard.
Platforms that let you publish articles or profiles with a followed link back to your site. All DA 80+. No registration fee required for basic submissions.
Podcast directories and media platforms. Getting your business featured or listed on these builds authority signals Google respects.
Industry resource pages, association directories, and niche citations. High editorial standards — links from these carry significant trust.
Answer questions, contribute expert knowledge, and link back naturally. These platforms reward genuine expertise — not spam.
After submitting, click Mark Submitted to move the prospect to your Won column and track your progress. All sites are manually reviewed — no link farms, PBNs, or paid link schemes.

DA Tier Guide
Tier 1 — DA 85+
Forbes, LinkedIn. Highest impact.
Tier 2 — DA 70-84
Industry publications. Best ROI.
Tier 3 — DA 50-69
Local news. Easier wins.
Tier 4 — DA 30-49
Community sites. Quick volume.

Indexing & AI Visibility
Get content found by Google, Bing, and the AI systems (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini). Each works differently and needs a different strategy.
Step-by-step guide: ① Google Search Console — paste your page URL and click Request Indexing. Google will crawl it within 1-4 days. ② Bing Webmaster — submit your sitemap URL once, then Bing auto-discovers new pages. ③ IndexNow — add your API key to instantly notify Bing, Yandex, and others every time you publish. One submission reaches multiple engines. ④ AI Visibility — submit your key pages to be included in AI training datasets so ChatGPT and Gemini learn about your business.
Google & Gemini
Crawls via Googlebot. Ranks by E-E-A-T and entity authority. Gemini uses Google's Knowledge Graph as primary training data.

Keys: Schema.org markup · Google Business Profile · Knowledge Panel · Search Console · Core Web Vitals
ChatGPT (OpenAI)
ChatGPT Search uses Bing's index. Training data = Common Crawl, Wikipedia. Getting into Bing fast = IndexNow protocol.

Keys: IndexNow · Bing Webmaster · Wikipedia citations · Wikidata entity · Bing Places
Claude (Anthropic)
Trained on Common Crawl, Wikipedia, books. No live search unless connected. Knowledge from training cutoff.

Keys: Wikipedia presence · Wikidata entity · Authoritative publications · Consistent NAP · Schema.org Organization
URL Submission Hub

Google Policy: Never offer incentives (discounts, gifts) in exchange for reviews — this violates Google's guidelines and can get the business suspended. These templates are policy-compliant.

Review Response Automation Paste any review → AI writes a personalised response in seconds
0 characters
Review Sentiment Analysis
Generated Response
Your AI-generated response will appear here. Paste a review and click Generate Response.
Response best practice: Always respond within 24 hours. A public response shows all future readers that you care about customer experience — not just the reviewer.
Response Variations — click to select
Response History (saved in browser)
Responded reviews will appear here for reference
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 5-Star Strategy
Thank them by name, mention the specific service, reinforce what they loved. Keep it genuine — not copy-pasted.
⭐⭐⭐⭐ 4-Star Strategy
Acknowledge the positive, express commitment to earning 5 stars next time, invite them to share what could be improved.
⭐–⭐⭐⭐ 1–3 Star Strategy
Apologise sincerely, take responsibility, move offline immediately. Never argue publicly. Other readers judge your reaction.

Voice Search & FAQ Optimisation
Voice queries are 3–5× longer than typed searches and almost always phrased as questions. These tools generate the content, schema, and keywords that get your clients into voice results on Google, Siri, Alexa, and AI assistants.
0
Questions
0
FAQ Pairs
0
Snippets
0
Schema Ready
Setup
Step 1 — Fill Your Business Details
Pull from your Business Profile or type below
Step 2 — Generate & Answer
Step 3 — Copy FAQ Schema below and paste into your website <head> tag
Submission Hub
Voice Search Submission Hub Where to deploy your FAQ schema & content
You cannot submit directly to voice assistants — they pull answers from your website schema, GBP, and directory listings. This hub shows you exactly where to deploy your FAQ content so every voice assistant can find it.
Google Assistant
Reads FAQ schema from your website and GBP posts. Copy the schema below and paste into every service page <head> tag. 90% of US voice searches go through Google.
Apple Siri
Siri pulls local business data from Apple Maps and Yelp. Claiming your Apple Maps listing is the direct submission path — no API needed.
Amazon Alexa
Alexa uses Bing for search and has a formal Yext partnership for local business data. Submit to Bing Places and sync via Yext for best coverage.
Bing / Cortana
Bing reads FAQ schema from your website and Bing Places for local data. Use the IndexNow API key already collected to notify Bing when schema is deployed.
Samsung Bixby
Bixby uses Google for web results and TripAdvisor / Yelp for local businesses. Optimising for Google covers Bixby automatically.
Google schema covers Bixby web results

Deployment Checklist — Do These in Order

FAQ Schema JSON-LD Paste into your <head> tag
Generate questions and answers to build FAQ schema.
Voice-Optimised GBP Checklist
Question & Answer Pairs 0 pairs
Fill in your services and city,
then click Generate Questions.
Featured Snippet Brief Builder
Voice assistants read the featured snippet aloud. 40–60 word direct answers to the top questions win Position Zero — and the voice result.
Snippet Composer Uses Anthropic key
Your 40–60 word snippet will appear here. Voice assistants read this verbatim.
Snippet Optimisation Tips
40–60 words — the sweet spot for featured snippets. Under 40 words lacks detail; over 60 words gets cut off in voice responses.
Start with the answer — lead with the direct response, then add supporting detail. Don't bury the lede.
Include the keyword — the exact phrase from the search query should appear naturally in the answer.
Add to FAQ Schema — wrap each Q&A pair in FAQPage JSON-LD. Google uses this to generate voice answers.
Mobile page speed — voice results are almost exclusively served from pages scoring 80+ on PageSpeed mobile.
HTTPS required — all voice result sources must be HTTPS. No exceptions across any platform.
Local Landing Page Builder
Generate complete, publish-ready landing pages for every service × city combination. Each page includes an SEO-optimised title, meta description, H1, intro, service bullets, FAQ section, schema markup, and a local CTA — in seconds.
How to add these pages to your WordPress site:
① Generate the page content using the form below
② Copy the generated HTML
③ In WordPress: go to Pages → Add New
④ Switch editor to Code Editor (top-right ⋮ menu) and paste the HTML
⑤ Set the URL slug to: city-service (e.g. "austin-plumber")
⑥ Recommended plugins: Yoast SEO (meta tags), Schema Pro (structured data), WP Rocket (speed)

What Changed & What Now Matters
❌ Deprecated (2024)
Google removed public Q&A posting from GBP. Users can no longer add or vote on Q&A directly in Business Profiles.
✅ What Works Now
FAQ schema (JSON-LD) on your website directly feeds Google Gemini and AI Overview answers. This tab now generates ready-to-deploy schema.
🎯 Action Priority
1. Generate FAQs below
2. Export JSON-LD schema
3. Add to every service page
4. Gemini uses it for AI answers

1
Open business.google.com and select your profile
2

Switch to your business account and answer the question with the AI-written answer

Content Type Legend & Best Practices
GBP Post
Post weekly. Use offers, events, updates. Include local keywords + photos.
Web 2.0
500–800 words. Link back to your site. Builds entity presence across platforms.
Blog
1,000–2,000 words. Target long-tail keywords. Add FAQ schema at the bottom.
Social
Facebook/Instagram. Repurpose GBP content. Use hashtags with city + service.
Email
Monthly newsletter. Highlight reviews, promotions, and seasonal services.

NAP Score
Canonical NAP (your source of truth)
Yext mode: Uses your Yext API key to pull live listing data and compare against your canonical NAP field by field.
Consistency Score
Run the audit to see your NAP consistency score
Audit Results
Fill in your canonical NAP and click Run NAP Audit
Common NAP Inconsistency Issues & How to Fix Them
❌ Phone Variations
Same number formatted differently: (512) 555-0100 vs 512-555-0100 vs 5125550100. Google treats these as different data points.
Fix: Standardise to (XXX) XXX-XXXX across all directories.
⚠ Address Variants
Street vs St vs St. — Suite 100 vs Ste 100 vs #100. These all signal different locations to Google.
Fix: Use USPS format — match exactly what appears on your Google Business Profile.
ℹ Name Variations
Acme Plumbing vs Acme Plumbing Co vs Acme Plumbing Company. Inconsistent names dilute citation authority.
Fix: Use the exact GBP name — no trademarked symbols unless on GBP itself.

Social Publisher — Blog 2 Social
Write once, publish everywhere. Compose a post, generate platform-optimised variations with AI, and push to Facebook Pages, LinkedIn Pages, and Google Business Profile simultaneously. Social tokens are stored locally — never sent to any third-party server.
Set up your business pages first:
• Facebook Business Page — go to facebook.com/pages/create · LinkedIn Company Page — linkedin.com/company/setup · Instagram Business · Twitter/X Account
Connecting: Add your Facebook Page Token and Page ID in the API Keys tab to enable direct publishing. For LinkedIn, add your LinkedIn Token. Without tokens, use the Copy button to paste content manually.

Platform Connections Tokens stored locally only
Facebook Page API
LinkedIn Pages API
Security: All tokens are stored in your browser's localStorage only — they never leave your device or get sent to any third-party server. Use Page Access Tokens (not personal tokens) with only the minimum required permissions: pages_manage_posts for Facebook, w_member_social for LinkedIn.

Click AI Adapt Per Platform to generate optimised versions for each platform.
Google Business Profile max 1,500 chars
Facebook Page max 63,206 chars (optimal: <280)

Platform Setup Guide
Google Business Profile
Uses your existing Google OAuth token from API Keys. Posts to your GBP location as a What's New / Offer / Event post. No extra setup required if your Google key is set.
Facebook Page
1. Go to developers.facebook.com
2. Open Graph API Explorer
3. Select your app → your Page → generate Page Access Token
4. Add permission: pages_manage_posts
5. Paste token + Page ID above
LinkedIn Page
1. Go to linkedin.com/developers/apps
2. Create an app → add your Company Page
3. Request permission: w_member_social
4. Generate OAuth 2.0 access token
5. Paste token above

Google Search Console Integration
Connect your Google OAuth key to pull real ranking data — keywords the site ranks for, average position, click-through rate, and impressions. Overlaid with your keyword matrix to show which generated keywords are already gaining traction in Google.
What Google Search Console shows you: The exact keywords people type into Google that lead them to your website, how many times your site appeared (impressions), how many people clicked, and your average position for each keyword. This is real data direct from Google — not estimates. To connect: Add your Google API key in API Keys tab, verify your website in Google Search Console (search.google.com/search-console), then click Fetch Data here.

Social Proof Aggregator
Social Proof Aggregator
Consolidates your review counts and ratings from Google, Yelp, Facebook, and BBB into a single Social Proof Score. Tracks month-over-month velocity so you can show clients their reputation is growing. A metric every client immediately understands.
What this does: Pulls review counts and star ratings from all your platforms (Google, Yelp, Facebook, BBB) and combines them into a single Social Proof Score out of 100. Use the Import button to pull from your Reputation tab, or enter counts manually. The score is calculated from: total review count (40%), average rating (35%), and monthly velocity (25%).

AI Agents
Autonomous multi-step workflows. Click Run to let the agent execute the full task sequence — each step is logged live as it runs.
How to use AI Agents: Each agent automates a specific SEO task. Run them individually by clicking the button on each card, or click Run All Agents to chain the full sequence. Agents read your Business Profile and live campaign data — the more complete your profile, the better the results. Add your Anthropic API key in the API Keys tab to enable AI-written content.
Agent running...
Run All Agents — Full Weekly SEO Sprint
Runs all 5 agents sequentially. Takes 2–5 minutes. Ideal to run once per week.
FEATURED
Weekly Client Report Agent Scheduled
Pulls all live scores and campaign data, writes an executive summary with Claude, builds a full branded HTML report, and emails it to your clients automatically on your chosen schedule.
All Tabs Claude AI Gmail Send Auto-Scheduler
Scheduler paused · Last sent: Never
The scheduler checks every minute while this browser tab is open. For fully automatic sending, keep the app open in a pinned browser tab.
Steps
1. Pull all live campaign data
2. Calculate score + week-over-week trend
3. Write exec summary (Claude)
4. Build full HTML report
5. Email to all recipients (Gmail)
Keyword Opportunity Spotter
Finds keywords ranking in positions 11–20 (page 2), cross-references your keyword matrix for gaps, then uses Claude to write a specific content brief for each — telling you exactly what to create or update to push them to page 1.
GSC Data Rank Tracker Matrix Gaps Content Briefs
Steps
1. Load GSC + rank tracker data
2. Filter positions 11–20 (page 2)
3. Cross-reference keyword matrix gaps
4. Write content brief per kw (Claude)
5. Build prioritised action list
GBP Health Monitor
Scores your Google Business Profile across 8 signals — posts, reviews, response rate, completeness, hours, and photos. If no post has been published in 7+ days, Claude drafts one automatically and queues it for publishing.
8 Signals Auto-Post Review Check Fix List
Steps
1. Load GBP posts + review data
2. Score 8 completeness signals
3. Check photo coverage
4. Check business hours
5. Auto-draft post if stale >7 days
6. Build fix list + AI summary
Review Velocity Agent
Measures your monthly review velocity against targets, checks average rating and response rate, identifies the best candidates for review requests, then uses Claude to write personalised SMS messages scored by likelihood of response — top 3 queued automatically.
Velocity Check Likelihood Score SMS Drafts Auto-Queue Top 3
Steps

3. Identify best candidates

5. Score likelihood + queue top 3
Multi-Location Sync Agent
Audits every location for citation coverage, NAP completeness, and schema readiness. Identifies the most lagging location, submits its top 10 missing DA directories, generates a LocalBusiness schema block for every location, and writes an AI sync report.
All Locations NAP Audit Auto-Submit Schema Gen
Steps
1. Load all location data
2. Audit citations + NAP per location
3. Identify most lagging location
4. Generate schema for all locations
5. Submit top 10 dirs for lagging loc
6. Write AI sync report
Local Links Prospector Agent
Generates fresh hyperlocal backlink prospects for your city and service — Chamber of Commerce, .edu and .gov links, local news, sponsor pages, and referral partners. Scores each by relevance, writes personalised outreach emails for the top 5 with Claude, and pushes them straight into the Backlink Pipeline.
7 Link Types Relevance Score AI Emails Auto-Pipeline
Steps
1. Load business + location data
2. Generate all 7 prospect types
3. Score by DA + type + city match
4. Write outreach emails — top 5 (Claude)
5. Push top 5 into backlink pipeline
Competitor Gap Agent
Runs a full 3-dimensional gap analysis against up to 3 competitors — citation coverage, keyword targeting, and backlink opportunities. Prioritises the 10 highest-impact gaps across all three dimensions and writes a Claude-powered tactical brief with exact actions ranked by ROI.
Citation Gap Keyword Gap Backlink Gap Top 10 Actions
Steps
1. Load competitor domains
2. Citation gap vs all 3 competitors
3. Keyword gap analysis
4. Backlink gap analysis
5. Prioritise top 10 cross-dimension
6. Write tactical brief (Claude)
Content Calendar Executor
Reads this week's calendar entries and executes them all at once — writes GBP posts with Claude and queues them to the Publisher, generates a full Web 2.0 article saved to the Web 2.0 tab, writes social posts pre-loaded into the Social Publisher, and drafts email newsletters ready to send.
GBP Posts Web 2.0 Article Social Posts Email Newsletter
Steps
1. Read this week's calendar items
2. Write GBP posts → queue Publisher
3. Write Web 2.0 article → save tab
4. Write social posts → Social Publisher
5. Write email newsletter
6. Queue all content + show results
Ping Submission Agent
Notifies Google, Bing, Yandex, IndexNow, and ChatGPT's index that your sitemap or new page is ready to crawl. Run this every time you publish new content to speed up indexing from days to hours.
Google Bing Yandex IndexNow
Steps
1. Read sitemap + page URL from Indexing tab
2. Submit pings to 5 search engines
3. Log results + update ping history
AUTO-SUBMIT
Citation Submission Scheduler Scheduled
Automatically submits your business to the highest-DA directories on a weekly, bi-weekly, or monthly schedule. Picks up where the last run left off — always working through the remaining directories in DA order.
Coverage Loading...
Scheduler paused
Keep this browser tab open for scheduled runs. Submits highest-DA directories first, working through the list each run.
Full SEO Audit Agent
Scores all 6 SEO signals, identifies top gaps, and writes a plain-English priority action list.
Steps
1. Pull all saved profile + campaign data
2. Score Profile, Citations, Backlinks, Web 2.0, Indexing, Local SEO
3. Identify top 5 gaps by ranking impact
4. Write executive summary (Claude AI)
5. Record score to history
Citation Builder Agent
Identifies unsubmitted directories, runs the Top 20 DA batch, then checks NAP consistency.
Steps
1. Count submitted vs pending directories
2. Select top 20 DA unsubmitted dirs
3. Run submission batch
4. Check NAP consistency across submitted dirs
5. Flag any mismatches, update citation score
Content Engine Agent
Picks 3 target keywords, writes a GBP post, a Web 2.0 article, and meta tags — all queued for review.
Steps
1. Read keyword matrix, pick 3 targets
2. Write GBP post for top keyword (Claude)
3. Write Web 2.0 article (Claude)
4. Generate meta tags for top service+city
5. Queue all content for review + publishing
Reputation Agent
Scans unanswered reviews, detects sentiment, drafts responses matched to star rating — queued for your approval.
Steps
1. Find all unanswered reviews
2. Detect sentiment + issues per review
3. Match tone to star rating automatically
4. Draft response for each (Claude)
5. Queue responses — you approve before posting
Schema + Voice Agent
Regenerates LocalBusiness schema, writes 15 voice Q&As, builds FAQPage schema, validates, and pings IndexNow.
Steps
1. Generate LocalBusiness JSON-LD from profile
2. Generate 15 voice Q&As (Claude)
3. Write answers for all Q&As (Claude)
4. Build + validate FAQPage schema
5. Ping IndexNow — Bing, Yandex, Naver
Agent Log
Live output from all agent runs this session
No agent runs yet — click Run on any agent above to begin.
Local Rank Tracker
Estimated keyword rankings for your top service + city combinations. Position estimates are based on your citation score, backlink profile, GBP activity, and content signals — not live SERP data. Updated when you click Refresh Rankings. Use as a directional metric, not a replacement for professional rank tracking tools.
How rankings work: Position 1 = top of Google. Positions 1-3 get ~60% of clicks. Positions 4-10 are page 1. Positions 11-20 are page 2 — these are your biggest opportunities (use the Keyword Opportunity Spotter agent). Rankings fluctuate daily — track trends over weeks, not days. Best keywords to track: city + service combos ("plumber Austin TX"), near-me variants, and your top 3 services.
